channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: After 60 days of war in Iran, does US Congress want a say?
item.description: US lawmakers may be reluctant to intervene in the Iran conflict, citing a 60-day window for Congressional approval to continue the war. Under the War Powers Resolution, President Trump must seek approval, but Congress may sidestep the issue.

item.title: US Fed expected to keep rates steady as Iran war effects ripple
item.description: The US Federal Reserve is expected to maintain interest rates, despite the economic impact of the Iran war. The Fed's decision will be closely watched as the conflict's effects ripple through the global economy.

item.title: Can the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty prevent a disaster?
item.description: The United Nations chief warns that the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty is losing influence. The global agreement aims to prevent nuclear disasters, but its effectiveness is waning. The United Nations chief warns of the waning influence of the global agreement.

item.title: Trump claims Tehran admitted it's in 'State of Collapse' - as UAE quits OPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has quit the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, citing a desire to diversify its economy. The move comes as US President Trump claims Iran has admitted to being in a state of collapse.

item.title: How a controversial gas pipeline deal could further stall Bosnia's EU accession
item.description: A US-backed gas pipeline deal could hinder Bosnia's bid to join the European Union. Brussels warns that a deal with a company linked to Trump allies could jeopardize the country's EU aspirations.

item.title: US Conditions for Paying U.N. Dues Include China Curbs -Report
item.description: The US has conditioned its payment of UN dues on curbs being placed on China's influence. This demand is part of a report on US contributions to the UN. US Conditions for Paying U. N.

item.title: U.K. PM Keir Starmer wins crunch Parliament vote over ethics inquiry
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer won a parliamentary vote against an ethics inquiry into his appointment of Peter Mandelson. The vote tally was 335 to 223. Members of Parliament voted 335 to 223 against a Privileges Committee investigation into whether he had misled the House of Commons over the appointment of disgraced peer Peter Mandelson.

item.title: France probes return of adult website linked to mass rape
item.description: French authorities are investigating the return of an adult website allegedly linked to a mass rape case. The probe is ongoing. France probes return of adult website linked to mass rape.

item.title: Austrian pleads guilty to ISIL-planned attack on Taylor Swift concert
item.description: Austrian man Beran A pleaded guilty to planning an ISIL-directed attack on Taylor Swift's Vienna concert. He also admitted to conspiring on other plans abroad. Beran A admits conspiring in other plans abroad as well as planning attack on Swift's Vienna concert.

item.title: Trump approval dips to record low amid Iran war, inflation woes: Poll
item.description: US President's approval rating hit a record low due to concerns over the Iran war and inflation. Only 22 percent of voters back his handling of the cost of living.

item.title: Elon Musk wanted 'keys to kingdom,' OpenAI lawyer says at trial
item.description: Elon Musk's lawyer testified that Musk wanted "keys to the kingdom" in his deal with OpenAI, implying significant control. Musk's expectations were not met, leading to a trial to resolve disputes over the agreement.

item.title: Why the UAE Walked Out on OPEC. and What It Means for the Cartel
item.description: The United Arab Emirates walked out of an OPEC meeting, citing disagreements over production quotas. The move signals growing tensions within the cartel and may impact OPEC's ability to coordinate oil production.

item.title: Iraq’s Prime Minister-Designate Thrust Into U.S.-Iran Power Struggle
item.description: Iraq's Prime Minister-designate, Ali al-Zaidi, faces pressure from the US to curb Iranian influence. He must form a new coalition government while navigating the complex US-Iran power struggle in the region.

item.title: Trump claims Iran 'just' informed U.S. it's in 'state of collapse'
item.description: Donald Trump claimed that Iran told the US it is in a "state of collapse". Ceasefire efforts appear stalled, with Iran resisting US attempts to dictate terms. Ceasefire efforts appears at a standstill, with Iran saying Washington could no longer dictate terms.

item.title: ‘Just peace’ can’t be found by joining organisations that charge fees: UNGA President defends UN
item.description: UN General Assembly President defended the UN against criticism. She argued that seeking a "just peace" requires working within the organization, not joining fee-charging groups. Ms.

item.title: They Lost Their Homes in Cuba to the Communist Government. Will They Ever Get Them Back?
item.description: Cuba's communist government faces compensation claims from people whose properties were seized decades ago. With Cuba in economic crisis, those affected say it's time to resolve the issue. With Cuba in dire economic crisis, people whose properties were seized by its government decades ago say it’s time to resolve thorny compensation claims.

item.title: "Was Even-Tempered, Calm": Former Classmate To NDTV On Trump Event Shooter
item.description: A former classmate of the Trump event shooter described him as "very calm and quiet" during their school days. The shooter was identified as Allen. Terlinden said that back when they shared classrooms, Allen was "very calm and quiet".

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Trump Dissatisfied With Iran Proposal To Lift Hormuz Blockade
item.description: US President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with Iran's proposal to lift the Hormuz blockade. The development is part of ongoing ceasefire talks between the US, Israel, and Iran. US Israel Iran War Ceasefire News LIVE Updates:.

item.title: 89-Year-Old Gunman Arrested After Mass Shooting In Athens, At Least 4 Wounded
item.description: An 89-year-old gunman was arrested after a mass shooting in Athens, wounding at least four people. The gunman later opened fire on a court building in another part of the city.

item.title: Prisoner Swap Between Belarus, Poland and Other Nations Frees 10
item.description: A prisoner swap involving multiple countries, including Belarus and Poland, resulted in the release of 10 people, including a well-known journalist. The exchange was facilitated by the Trump administration's outreach to Belarus.
